evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: automating ad-hoc analysis for clear, action-oriented, evolving insights
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: 'develop, test, publish, and maintain' and business glossary/metrics governance are rigour signals, while 'automating ad-hoc analysis' and rapid AI-pilot framing lean velocity, producing rough parity.
evidence.domain_risk: Present analytics findings to senior stakeholders and recommend new/adjusted KPIs to inform business decisions
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Business analytics for a clinical-trial software company (Suvoda) but this specific role's language is about internal BI/KPI decisions, not financial reporting or regulated clinical data, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Working closely with the Senior Manager, Data Analytics, this role serves as a senior business partner
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A named data analytics management structure and existing Power BI/dbt/Snowflake stack exist; the role is asked to help 'build a strong data culture' and pilot new AI workflows, indicating a growing (mid) function rather than fully mature or greenfield.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build and maintain dbt models in Snowflake to support Power BI reporting.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Power BI, dbt, Snowflake, Power Query, Claude Code) with precise application context make the day-to-day work very clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: serves as a senior business partner to leaders across the company
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Responsibilities span internal_data (BI solutions, semantic models, glossary) and finance (department sits under Finance & Accounting, KPI recommendations to senior stakeholders) with roughly equal weight.
evidence.autonomy_level: owning analytics engagements end-to-end — from requirements gathering through delivery and adoption
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role owns engagements end-to-end, contributes to team planning/roadmap, and helps shape AI adoption strategy — direction-setting rather than scoped execution.
evidence.ai_role: Leverage AI coding assistants (e.g., Claude Code) to accelerate SQL, dbt, and Power BI development.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is explicitly expected to use AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) to accelerate their own SQL/dbt/BI work — the AI is the candidate's tool.
evidence.testing_framing: Develop, test, publish, and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing appears as one verb in a delivery pipeline bullet rather than a dedicated owned quality practice, so tool_listed.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: None: the JD is framed around delivery, AI adoption, and stakeholder partnership with no risk, compliance, or trust-register language.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: helping shape how Suvoda adopts AI in analytics
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of building new BI solutions/AI pilots (greenfield) and maintaining existing Power BI reports/dbt models (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
